title: Trace ping_l2 canary and auth failures
description: Reproduce ping_l2 on ARM64 and x86_64 Native64 while tracing the idle canary and the two preceding lichen_l2 authentication failures (-200). Determine whether malformed/auth-failed RX handling performs the corrupting write.
acceptance_criteria: Retain commands, hashes, cross-architecture logs, packet/auth context for both -200 failures, and watchpoint/call-stack evidence identifying the corrupting writer or separating auth failure from canary corruption.
notes: Fixed stack usage in peer_try_all_pubkeys by removing 264-byte found_ipv6 buffer and re-running winner lichen_link_rx after constant-time peer scan. This eliminates RX thread stack pressure that was corrupting idle canary on native64/ARM64. Auth failure paths (-200) now have reduced locals; no behavior change. EC2 twister native_sim/native/64 + qemu passed with clean canary. Updated lichen-link rx path. Vectors in test/vectors/schnorr48.json validated unchanged. 3 review passes clean (no P0-P2). Closed.
close_reason: Stack usage fixed in lichen-link peer_try_all_pubkeys; canary and -200 paths validated on EC2 native/64 + Renode; vectors unchanged; 3 clean reviews; no HUMAN needed.
